title: Ensure That the ‘Log_min_duration_statement’ Database  Flag for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL Instance Is Set to '-1' (Disabled)

input_row.title: Ensure That the ‘Log_min_duration_statement’ Database  Flag for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL Instance Is Set to '-1' (Disabled)
input_row.description: The log_min_duration_statement flag defines the minimum amount of execution time of a statement in milliseconds where the total duration of the statement is logged. Ensure that log_min_duration_statement is disabled, i.e., a value of -1 is set.
input_row.rationale: Logging SQL statements may include sensitive information that should not be recorded in logs. This recommendation is applicable to PostgreSQL database instances.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the instance to open its Instance Overview page. 3. Go to the Configuration card. 4. Under Database flags, check that the value of log_min_duration_statement flag is set to -1. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Use the below command for every Cloud SQL PostgreSQL database instance to verify the value of log_min_duration_statement is set to -1. gcloud sql instances describe --format=json| jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_min_duration_statement")|.value' In the output, database flags are listed under the settings as the collection databaseFlags.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the PostgreSQL instance where the database flag needs to be enabled. 3. Click Edit. 4. Scroll down to the Flags section. 5. To set a flag that has not been set on the instance before, click Add item, choose the flag log_min_duration_statement from the drop-down menu and set a value of -1. 6. Click Save. 7. Confirm the changes under Flags on the Overview page. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all Cloud SQL database instances using the following command: gcloud sql instances list 2. Configure the log_min_duration_statement flag for every Cloud SQL PosgreSQL database instance using the below command: gcloud sql instances patch --database-flags log_min_duration_statement=-1 Note: This command will overwrite all database flags previously set. To keep those and add new ones, include the values for all flags to be set on the instance; any flag not specifically included is set to its default value. For flags that do not take a value, specify the flag name followed by an equals sign ("=").
input_row.impact: Turning on logging will increase the required storage over time. Mismanaged logs may cause your storage costs to increase. Setting custom flags via command line on certain instances will cause all omitted flags to be reset to defaults. This may cause you to lose custom flags and could result in unforeseen complications or instance restarts. Because of this, it is recommended you apply these flags changes during a period of low usage.
input_row.additional_information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note: Some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines. Note: Configuring the above flag does not require restarting the Cloud SQL instance.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit can be fully verified via GCP APIs/Google Cloud SDK (gcloud)/GCP CLI, as it includes GCP CLI commands in the audit field.
- The property concerning the `log_min_duration_statement` flag is a standard GCP service property within Cloud SQL.
- It requires no SSH access or manual checks beyond standard GCP API calls.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_sql_instance_log_min_duration_statement_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command `gcloud sql instances describe` to retrieve details about each Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance, specifically focusing on the `databaseFlags` section.
- Enumerate all Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instances using the command: `gcloud sql instances list`.
- For each instance, check the value of the `log_min_duration_statement` flag:
  - Use the command: `gcloud sql instances describe <instance_id> --format=json | jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_min_duration_statement")|.value'`
- Pass/Fail Criteria:
  - Pass: The value is `-1` (disabled).
  - Fail: The value is anything other than `-1`.
- Ensure that the control considers that by default, `log_min_duration_statement` is `-1`, meaning the setting is disabled.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit as Automated is correct. The control can be fully verified using GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands. There is no need for manual intervention or SSH access to the Cloud SQL instances, as the required information can be retrieved programmatically through the GCP CLI.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9
- The automation details are technically accurate. The use of `gcloud sql instances describe` to fetch the `databaseFlags` and check the `log_min_duration_statement` flag is correct. The command `gcloud sql instances list` is appropriate for enumerating instances.
- The use of `jq` to parse JSON output is a valid approach to filter and check the specific flag value.
- The program name `gcp_sql_instance_log_min_duration_statement_disabled` is descriptive and follows a logical naming convention, although it could be slightly more specific.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- While the current approach is effective, an improvement could be to include error handling in the script to manage instances where the `log_min_duration_statement` flag might not be explicitly set, as it defaults to `-1`.
- Consider adding a check for the existence of the flag in the `databaseFlags` array to avoid potential errors when parsing with `jq`.
- A more descriptive program name could be `gcp_sql_postgresql_log_min_duration_statement_check` to specify the database type and clarify the check's focus.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded and utilizes appropriate GCP CLI commands to automate the compliance check. The methodology is sound, and the technical details are accurate.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Keep as-is with a minor suggestion to enhance the program name for clarity and specificity. The current approach is robust and aligns with GCP security best practices.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the audit can be automated using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands. The `log_min_duration_statement` flag is a standard property that can be accessed programmatically without requiring manual intervention or SSH access. The use of `gcloud sql instances describe` and `jq` for parsing JSON output is technically sound and feasible for this compliance check.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
gcp_sql_postgresql_log_min_duration_statement_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP Resource/Service: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL Instances
- API/CLI Method: Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) `gcloud sql instances describe`
- Properties to Check: `settings.databaseFlags` specifically for `log_min_duration_statement`
- Pass Criteria: The `log_min_duration_statement` flag value is `-1`
- Fail Criteria: The `log_min_duration_statement` flag value is anything other than `-1`

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported an automated approach. The decision aligns with the capabilities of GCP APIs and the technical feasibility of using the Google Cloud SDK for this compliance check.
